{{Short description|Compilation album series by various artists}} {{List to table|date=August 2025}} {{Italic title|string=Now That's What I Call Music!}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2020}} {{Infobox album | italic_title = no | name = Now That's What I Call Music! | type = Compilation | longtype = series | artist = various artists | cover = NOW Thats What I Call Music logo.png | alt = | caption = This logo format has been used on numbered releases since ''Now That's What I Call Music! 20'', first released in November 1991. | released = 28 November 1983 | genre = Various artists | label = [[Universal Music Group]], [[Sony Music]] | prev_title = | prev_year = | next_title = | next_year = }} This is a list of available physical albums belonging to the official 1983 ''[[Now That's What I Call Music!]]'' UK series, comprising [[compact disc]]s (CD), magnetic audio [[Cassette tape|cassettes]] (AC), [[Phonograph record|vinyl]] ([[LP record|LP]]), [[VHS]] tape, [[DVD]] and other short-lived formats. They are categorized by series (country), then ordered by date. All countries but the United Kingdom have discontinued their respective series. {{TOC limit|2}} == United Kingdom and Ireland== The evolution of ''Now That's What I Call Music!'' in the UK reflects the changing landscape of music consumption formats. Starting with vinyl and magnetic audio cassettes, the series adapted to the rise of compact discs (CDs) and explored various formats like DVDs, MiniDiscs, and videos. === Music formats === The initial releases of ''Now That's What I Call Music!'' in the UK and Ireland were exclusively on vinyl magnetic audio cassette and laserdisc.{{Cite web |title=Various - Now That's What I Call Music {{!}} Releases |url=https://www.discogs.com/master/72065-Various-Now-Thats-What-I-Call-Music |access-date=2024-02-18 |website=[[Discogs]] |date=1983 }} ==== Compact discs (CD) ==== * The series transitioned to CD with the release of ''Now That's What I Call Music 4'' in a single-CD format in 1984.{{cite web|title=NOW That's What I Call Music! 4|url=https://superdeluxeedition.com/news/now-4-to-be-issued-on-cd-in-nov/|website=Superdeluxeedition.com|date=11 October 2019 |access-date=25 July 2024}} * Subsequent single-CD versions, such as ''Now That's What I Call Music 8'' and ''Now That's What I Call Music 9'', followed. * Double-CD releases commenced with ''Now That's What I Call Music 10'' in November 1987. ==== Phonograph records ==== * [[Phonograph record]] production concluded with ''Now That's What I Call Music! 35'' in November 1996.{{cite web |last=Hogan |first=Michael |date=21 May 2013 |title=Was Now That's What I Call Music! the first album you ever bought? |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/tvandradio/10069679/Was-Now-Thats-What-I-Call-Music-the-first-album-you-ever-bought.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220111/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/tvandradio/10069679/Was-Now-Thats-What-I-Call-Music-the-first-album-you-ever-bought.html |archive-date=11 January 2022 |access-date=23 September 2017 |website=The Telegraph}}{{cbignore}} * Special vinyl editions like ''Now That's What I Call Punk and New Wave'' emerged in the 21st century.{{Cite web |date=3 December 2021 |title=Now That's What I Call Punk & New Wave |url=https://superdeluxeedition.com/news/now-thats-what-i-call-punk-new-wave/ |access-date=26 May 2023 |website=Superdeluxeedition.com}} ==== Magnetic audio cassettes ==== * Magnetic audio cassettes ended with ''Now That's What I Call Music! 64'' in July 2006.{{cite web |last=Wade |first=Ian |date=27 November 2013 |title=Hit By Hit: 30 Years Of Now That's What I Call Music |url=http://thequietus.com/articles/13978-now-thats-what-i-call-music |access-date=23 September 2017 |website=The Quietus}}{{cite web |last= |first= |date=10 July 2018 |title=25 Facts about the Now That's What I Call Music Compilation Albums...|url=https://www.totalmk.co.uk/music/25-facts-about-the-now-thats-what-i-call-music-compilation-albums |access-date=1 July 2024|website=totalmk}} ==== MiniDiscs ==== * The MiniDisc format inclusion began with ''Now That's What I Call Music! 43'' in July 1999, and concluded in April 2001 with ''Now That's What I Call Music! 48''.{{cite web |date=28 September 2020 |title=Now That's What I Call Music! 48 |url=https://musicbrainz.org/release-group/1f8ca139-4a54-3953-a436-75b2e258dcb4 |access-date=28 September 2020 |website=musicbrainz.org}} ==== Video releases ==== * ''Now That's What I Call Music!'' UK volumes 1 through 18 and ''Now That's What I Call Music! 20'' were individually released on VHS tapes.{{cite web |date=24 July 2023 |title=NOW That's What I Call Music 2006 - The UMD |url=https://www.pinterest.co.uk/nowmusic/now-thats-what-i-call-a-vhs/ |access-date=30 June 2024 |website=musicbrainz.org}} * The first two albums were also issued on [[LaserDisc|LaserDiscs]]. * From 2001 to 2007, videos transitioned to DVD, with an annual release.{{cite web |date=30 July 2023 |title=NOW That's What I Call Music 2006 - The UMD |url=https://www.pinterest.co.uk/nowmusic/now-thats-what-i-call-a-dvd/ |access-date=24 July 2023 |website=musicbrainz.org}} * DVDs are numbered by year, unrelated to album numbers. * ''Now 2006'' was released on UMD as well as DVD. === Release dates === The release dates mentioned pertain to the United Kingdom. Until the release of ''Now That's What I Call Music! 90'' on 30 March 2015, the release date in [[Ireland]] preceded the UK release by 3 days. However, starting with ''Now That's What I Call Music! 91'' on 24 July 2015, the release dates in the United Kingdom and Ireland have been simultaneous, aligning with the [[Global Release Day]] campaign by [[International Federation of the Phonographic Industry|IFPI]], effective from 10 July 2015.{{cite web |title="New Music Fridays" are coming – Global release day launches 10th July |url=http://www.ifpi.org/news/New-Music-Fridays-are-coming |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170326005728/http://ifpi.org/news/New-Music-Fridays-are-coming/ |archive-date=26 March 2017 |access-date=14 November 2021 |website=Ifpi.org}} {|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- ! rowspan=2|Volume ! rowspan=2|Release date ! colspan=2|Peak chart positions ! rowspan=2|Certification{{cite certification|region=United Kingdom|title=What I Call Music|access-date=13 May 2023}} |- ! From the release of ''Now 102'' in 2019 until ''Now 118'' in 2024, the corresponding ''Now'' album from 100 volumes prior would be re-compiled and re-issued on double-CD sets on the same day. Due to licensing issues, these re-compiled versions sometimes use alternative mixes such as album versions. Originally, the series captured extended 12" mixes of dance hits of the time, but from 1991 onwards, all ''Now Dance'' compilations featured 7" edits with only occasional extended versions or mixes included. The first two volumes were not released on compact disc, and all subsequent releases were either single-, double-, or triple-disc sets. Unlike the main series, ''Now Dance'' were originally titled and numbered by their year of release (i.e. ''Now Dance '89''). The frequency with which they were released also varied from year to year; initially, and most frequently across the series, there would be one ''Now Dance'' volume per year, but in 1994 for example, four volumes were released, and in 1996, no ''Now Dance'' compilations were released. Towards the end of the series, 3-CD [[digipak]] sets were issued, but in 2010, the long-running title was retired, and all subsequent dance-themed ''Now'' collections have been issued under the ''Special Editions'' series. In 2021, the original concept of ''Now Dance'' was revived with a new series of compilations containing 12" remixes released under the title ''Now That's What I Call 12" 80s''. In February 2023, 12" mixes all taken from the first three ''Now Dance'' compilations, originally released in 1985, 1986 and 1989, were collected on a special 4CD and 3-LP set, ''Now Dance: The 80s'', with artwork inspired by the original ''Now Dance 86''. These compilations are similar to the anniversary series, but they contain fewer tracks and retailed for much less than the former series. The first sixteen volumes were released on the same date, and the final four issued four months later. They are the first time a ''Now'' series had released a compilation of hits from the years 1980 to 1982. An earlier compilation tie-in with ''Smash Hits'' from 1987 did, however, include tracks from those years. ''Now That's What I Call Timeless - The Eternal Collection'' (30 January 2026) 4-CD and 3-LP stunning orange vinyl selection # ''Now That's What I Call 70s Soul'' (coming 24 April 2026) 4-CD and 3-LP beautiful blue vinyl selection === Now Yearbook series (2021-present) === The ''Yearbook'' series are 4-CD and 3-LP vinyl sets, each representing a year of music, which launched in June 2021. Each release has a tie-in block of programming on ''[[Now 80s]]''. Each release is issued as a limited edition mini-hardback book, with the CDs in sleeves forming the pages of the '[[yearbook]]' which contains an introductory overview of the year, original 7" single artwork, trivia, chart positions and a quiz. The limited edition 3-LP version is released on bright coloured vinyl. A standard 4-CD version is also issued in a gatefold "wallet" design, which retails cheaper than the mini-hardback books. The first collection focuses on hits from 1983, the birth year of the ''Now That's What I Call Music!'' series. The year 1984 followed, but after this, the series rewound its year of focus, issuing collections that went from 1982 and backwards into the late 1970s. A triple-CD only release, ''Yearbook Extra: The Collectors Edition'', is issued a few weeks later; these include lesser known tracks and more songs by big artists included on the main album and they are released only in standard gatefold wallet packaging. A further release, ''The Final Chapter'', a deluxe 4-CD and 3-LP set, was issued in December 2022, rounding off the ''Yearbook'' years 1980 to 1984. The same month, a deluxe 5-LP [[box set]], ''Now Yearbook 1980-1984: Vinyl Extra'', was released. This was the first time tracks from the ''Extra'' CD series had been issued on vinyl. Another strand of the ''Now Yearbook'' series, in August 2022, is the somewhat experimental release of a limited edition, [[extended play]], 7" [[vinyl single]], containing 2 tracks each from the 1983 and 1984 ''Yearbook''s. This is the first time the ''Now That's What I Call Music!'' brand has released music commercially on 7" vinyl. The ''Now Yearbook'' series continues both forwards, with ''Now Yearbook 1985'' issued in November 2022, and backwards, with the release of ''Now Yearbook 1979'' in September 2022 (an ''Extra'' followed in October). The latter contains a new retro-'70s ''Now'' logo design and artwork which complements its '80s sister ''Yearbook'' series. The series then moved forward again, with the release of ''Now Yearbook 1992'' in July 2023 (an ''Extra'' followed in August). The 1990s Yearbook series contains a new retro-'90s logo design and artwork which also complements its sister decade series. The tie-in television programmes are a month-by-month video playlist of tracks, from one year previously featured on a ''Now Yearbook'' album, with [[Bruno Brookes]], [[Simon Bates]] and [[Jenny Powell]] presenting.[https://www.teleboy.ch/en/tv-guide/now80s/23417744/the-now-yearbook-1980] {{dead link|date=May 2023}}{{cite web | url=https://tv24.co.uk/p/the-now-yearbook-1983-qqdevo | title=The NOW Yearbook 1983, TV }} The ''Yearbook'' series was introduced to the ''Vault'' series, an album series with songs that are lesser known in the UK, but are way better known in the US and other countries (e.g. [["Weird Al" Yankovic]]'s "[[Eat It]]" peaked at #36 in the UK, but was a bigger hit in America (#12), Canada (#5), New Zealand (#6) and in Australia (where it reached #1)). # Due to the higher cost of pressing vinyl and the limited time they are available, they cost at least three times their compact-disc counterparts. They are mostly pressed on black vinyl as standard although some are released on coloured vinyl.
